name: Tashkent
description: Tashkent is Uzbekistan’s capital — Central Asia’s largest city, political, economic and cultural hub. Population over 3.1 million (2025), area ~435 km², 12 districts. Ancient city (2,200+ years) and modern aviation/transport node. Continental climate; honoured as CIS Youth Capital 2024; embassies, industry, theatres, museums, Humo Arena and Tashkent City define its profile.

monuments.title: Hazrati Imam (Hastimom)
monuments.info: Major religious and architectural ensemble in Old Tashkent (Olmazor), named after scholar Qaffol Shoshiy; holds the oldest Osman Quran; includes Barak Khan Madrasa, Tilla Sheikh Mosque and Qaffol Shoshiy mausoleum.

monuments.title: Kukeldash Madrasa
monuments.info: 16th c. (c. 1570), Shaybanid era — one of Tashkent’s largest madrasas beside Chorsu, long a centre of learning.

monuments.title: Chorsu Bazaar (Old City)
monuments.info: Historic “Old Juva” market under a blue dome at four trading streets; crafts, food and Old Tashkent atmosphere.

monuments.title: Sheikhantaur Mausoleum
monuments.info: 14th c. memorial to Sufi Sheikh Khovandi Tahur; two chambers under a dome; sacred plane tree nearby; part of an ensemble with Qaldirghochbiy and Yunus Khan.

monuments.title: Suzuk Ota complex
monuments.info: Mausque and mausoleum (1363–64) for Suzuk Ota, descendant of Khoja Ahmad Yasawi, built by Timur; centre of craft traditions; restored with craft workshops around it.

monuments.title: Zangiota complex
monuments.info: 12th–14th c. pilgrimage site in Zangiota District: mausoleums of saint Zangi Ota and Ambar Bibi, mosque, madrasa and gardens from Timurid times.

monuments.title: Ming Urik site
monuments.info: Ancient urban ruins (1st–13th c. AD) by the Salor canal near the railway — likely capital of ancient Chach; walls and dwellings; named for a 19th c. apricot orchard.

monuments.title: Shoshtepa site
monuments.info: 9th–8th c. BC settlement by the Jon canal, among Tashkent’s earliest archaeology; capped for conservation; linked to the city’s 2,200-year narrative.

monuments.title: Aktepa site (Yunusabad)
monuments.info: 5th–13th c. fortress mound by the Aktapa canal; early medieval stronghold of the Shash oasis; conservation and future tourism planned.

monuments.title: Sheikh Zainuddin Mausoleum
monuments.info: 13th–14th c. for Sheikh Zainuddin Kuvayi Arifon; mosque, cemetery and famous chillakhona with remarkable acoustics.

monuments.title: Khoja Ahror Valiy Mosque
monuments.info: 15th c. Friday mosque built by Khoja Ahror, once the city’s main jome; three large domes; linked to the arrival of the Osman Quran.

monuments.title: Yunus Khan Mausoleum
monuments.info: After 1487, for Moghulistan ruler Yunus Khan, built by his son Ahmad Khan; rectangular plan, iwan and khanaka; part of the Sheikhantaur cemetery.

monuments.title: Abdulkasim Madrasa
monuments.info: 19th c. near Oliy Majlis; major school where Abdulla Qodiriy and Munavvar Qori studied; now the Craftsmen’s Union with workshops for visitors.

monuments.title: Qaldirghochbiy Mausoleum
monuments.info: 15th c. pyramid-like dome in Sheikhantaur cemetery — one of the oldest structures there.

monuments.title: Barak Khan Madrasa
monuments.info: c. 1531–32 by Navruz Ahmad Khan (Barak Khan) in Hazrati Imam; brick madrasa with Shaybanid tombs; central to the ensemble.
